title: trifolium glanduliferum subsp. nervulosum
intro: Trifolium glanduliferum subsp. nervulosum, also known as glandular clover, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the legume family, characterized by its small yellow flowers and glandular leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The herb can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, glandular clover has been used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and respiratory issues.
